node.statement: (BP for Cuntz algebras — type collapse) Let a_1, ..., a_m ∈ (O_n)_{sa} and C = {φ ∈ S(O_n) : φ(a_j) = b_j}. If φ is extreme in C, then dim_R(π_φ(O_n)')_{sa} ≤ m + 1. In particular, π_φ is type I (direct sum of finitely many irreducibles with finite multiplicities), despite generic states on O_n yielding type III factors. Proof: Follows from Corollary BP for C*-algebras (node 1.6) applied to A = O_n. The type I conclusion follows from finite-dimensionality of the commutant.
